skill.name: invoice-organizer
skill.description: Organizes invoices and receipts for tax preparation by extracting key details, applying consistent filenames, sorting files into folders, and creating a summary for accounting review.
skill.summary: Organizes invoices and receipts into tax-ready folders with standardized names and a summary CSV.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many backtick and weak-cryptography matches, but review found those hits are Markdown examples and prose, not Ruby execution or cryptographic code. The skill is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it instructs the assistant to scan folders, copy or move financial documents, and optionally create a persistent organization script.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Commands Can Reorganize Financial Documents
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill instructs the assistant to find invoice files and, after approval, create directories and copy or move original invoice files. This is legitimate for an invoice organizer, but mistakes could misfile, duplicate, or move sensitive financial records, so users should review the proposed plan before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain concrete shell examples for find, mkdir, cp, and mv. The intent is benign organization, but these commands directly affect user files.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Folder Watch Automation Requires Review
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The automation section asks for a script that watches a downloads folder and auto-organizes new invoice files. This can be useful, but persistent file monitoring can repeatedly alter financial documents if implemented without safeguards.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill requests a watcher script in plain language but does not provide implementation code. Risk depends on the generated script and user approval process.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Static Matches Are Mostly Markdown
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for the Ruby/shell backtick interpretation. The listed matches are Markdown code fences, inline filenames, prompt examples, CSV samples, and folder trees. Real shell examples are documented separately as medium-risk filesystem operations, not hidden Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited SKILL.md ranges shows Markdown documentation syntax and user-facing examples. No Ruby source file or backtick command substitution logic is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The cited lines contain metadata, invoice field names, filename examples, CSV examples, folder trees, or prose. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing APIs, encryption, or password handling in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each weak-cryptography location was checked against the Markdown file and none contains crypto-related code. The analyzer appears to have matched ordinary text such as description fields or Markdown content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands for File Discovery and Movement
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash examples that find invoice files and copy or move them into an organized folder structure. These commands should run only after the user reviews the planned file changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and affect local files, but they are standard file-management commands used for the stated purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Suggested Persistent Automation Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests creating a script that watches a downloads folder and organizes new invoice files automatically. This pattern needs user review because persistent automation can continue changing files after setup.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file contains a prompt for automation, but no actual watcher implementation. The risk is contextual and depends on generated code.

content.value_statement: Messy invoice folders slow down tax preparation and expense review. This skill reads invoice files, extracts key details, renames documents, and sorts them into clean folders.
actual_capabilities: Scans a folder for invoice and receipt files such as PDFs, images, and screenshots.
Extracts common invoice details including vendor, date, invoice number, amount, and description.
Builds consistent filenames using date, vendor, document type, and description fields.
Organizes copies or moved files by vendor, year, category, quarter, or tax category.
Creates a summary CSV for accountants, reimbursement review, or accounting imports.
Flags documents for manual review when key invoice details cannot be extracted.
limitations: Extraction accuracy depends on document quality, OCR results, and invoice layout clarity.
It is not tax, legal, or accounting advice and cannot decide deductibility by itself.
File moves and copies require user review to avoid misfiling important records.
Automation scripts need separate review before running continuously on a downloads folder.

use_cases.title: Prepare Annual Tax Records
use_cases.description: Turn a messy folder of receipts and invoices into a year-based archive with a CSV for an accountant.

use_cases.title: Reconcile Monthly Expenses
use_cases.description: Sort recent business receipts by category and create a monthly expense summary for review.

use_cases.title: Clean Repeated Invoice Downloads
use_cases.description: Rename generic files such as invoice.pdf using vendor, date, and service details from each document.

prompt_templates.title: Organize a Receipt Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Organize the invoices and receipts in this folder. Show me the proposed folder structure before changing files.

prompt_templates.title: Create Tax-Ready Folders
prompt_templates.prompt: Read all invoices here, extract date, vendor, amount, and description, then organize copies by year and expense category.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Accountant Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a summary CSV with date, vendor, invoice number, amount, category, and final file path for every invoice.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Ongoing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reviewed workflow for new invoices in my downloads folder. Include safeguards, approval steps, and rollback guidance.

output_examples.input: A folder contains mixed PDFs and receipt images from software, office, and travel vendors.
output_examples.output: A proposed folder plan grouped by year and category, with sample renamed files and a list of documents needing review.

output_examples.input: Several files are named invoice.pdf, invoice(1).pdf, and receipt.jpg.
output_examples.output: Files are renamed with date, vendor, document type, and service description, then grouped under vendor folders.

output_examples.input: A manager needs last month expenses prepared for reimbursement review.
output_examples.output: A month summary lists total processed receipts, category totals, vendor counts, and the generated spreadsheet location.

best_practices: Review the proposed rename and folder plan before approving file changes.
Keep original invoice files or backups until the organized archive is verified.
Use a consistent folder strategy that matches your accounting or tax workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not run bulk moves before checking a representative sample of extracted details.
Do not rely on OCR results alone for tax-critical amounts or dates.
Do not create persistent automation without safeguards, logs, and a rollback plan.

faq.question: Can this skill organize scanned receipts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can read visible text from image receipts when OCR quality is good enough.

faq.question: Does it modify my original files?
faq.answer: The skill recommends copying files by default and moving files only if the user prefers that approach.

faq.question: Can it create a spreadsheet for my accountant?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create a CSV summary with dates, vendors, invoice numbers, amounts, categories, and file paths.

faq.question: Can it determine tax deductibility?
faq.answer: It can organize by tax category when instructed, but a qualified professional should verify tax treatment.

faq.question: What happens when details are missing?
faq.answer: The skill flags unclear documents for manual review and can use filename or file date clues as fallbacks.

faq.question: Is it safe to automate new invoice handling?
faq.answer: Automation is possible, but scripts should include review steps, logs, and backup behavior before continuous use.
